The walls of the barn are made of broad planks and slightly spaced apart to let
the fresh air circulate freely from the outside. At one end of the barn there
are large doors leading out; and at the other, a small door leading to the
living quarters. A huge haystack towers in the corner of the barn, and a small,
blue cart stands next to it. The other side of the barn is filled with animal
stalls.
name: Well-kept Barn

desc:
The farmhouse has been split into two parts. One part is filled with crates full
of carrots, potatoes and other vegetables. Next to them, a neat collection of
various gardening tools has been arranged on the back wall. The other part of
the house, separated partly by a wall and partly by curtains, contains a stove,
with a kettle on it, and other furniture usually found in small homes.
name: Wooden Farmhouse

desc:
An overgrown ruin, half buried in the high grass, stands within a bushy thicket.
A long time ago, there was a partly wooden barn standing on a firm foundation of
stones. Now, the building has collapsed inward into a pile of rotten planks
covered with ivy. The lone southern wall still stands upright, being the last
sign of the construction. Around the ruins, the landscape has been retaken by
bushes too dense to travel through.
name: Overgrown Ruins

desc:
Dense vegetation covers the soil, among the faint remains of the low irrigation
ditches that mark the presence of former agricultural developments. Near one of
these ditches, a small, ruined shelter, the kind that usually was built for
livestock, has been built between the bushes. The shelter consists of a few
rocks, planks, and poles, and has now been overgrown with nettles and brambles.
name: Bushy Plains near the Road

desc:
The Old Forest Road runs along the tree line, following the curves of the gloomy
forest, located on the north side of the road. The surface of the road is rough;
sometimes there are medium sized stones; sometimes there are deep ruts in the
road. On the border of the forest, large trees and the messy undergrowth have
grown so tightly next to each other that it is nearly impossible to enter the
forest. On the south side of the road lie vast, feral plains.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The outskirts of the northern forest frame the road. In general, the road is in
proper condition, although occasional grass tufts and even small bushes grow
here and there on it. Based on the condition of the road, it is evident that
only few, if any, travellers frequent the road nowadays. The surrounding terrain
is not particularly harsh, and one could travel easily in any direction from
here.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The tall trees suddenly part into a forest clearing, where weeds and some small
bushes grow. Winding between the trees, a small forest trail meets another path
in the clearing where an odd looking stone, covered with green and grey moss,
stands at the crossing. The forest opens up to the south, while in other
directions it gets denser.
name: Small Forest Glade

desc:
The grassy plain is almost like a canyon between the two forest walls, which are
standing at the both ends of the open area. Although the dark forest to the west
is big and truly majestic, it is still nothing compared to the endless Mirkwood,
which extends into unknown distances in the eastern horizon. The Old Forest Road
runs to the south.
name: Fields by the Forest Wall

desc:
The forest has suffered a bitter loss here, as if someone has been cutting trees
down throughout the area. In the middle of a small opening, an especially
enormous tree has fallen and only a gigantic stump remains where it once stood.
The stump has a strong, thick bark which is covered with fungi. However, the
upper part of the stump is clear, showing hundreds and hundreds of growth rings.
name: A Giant Stump

desc:
Low undergrowth fills the plains, allowing excellent visibility in all
directions. The only exceptions to this pattern are the lonely bushes and
occasional larger rocks that rise upwards from the ground. Here and there,
animal tracks run back and forth on the ground. To the north, the Old Forest
Road is located between the plains and the forest. In other directions, the
lonesome expanse of plains can be seen.
name: Plains by the Road

desc:
The sloping landscape is covered with brambles and other shrubs. The surrounding
area can be seen easily from this high perch in the terrain. To the northeast, a
road can be spotted. Further to the east, the border of the dark forest of
Mirkwood fills the whole eastern horizon. A tangled mess of stinging bushes
spreads out to the west and south. Behind the bushes to the far south, a few
high hills are visible in the distance.
name: High Ground

desc:
The wild scrubland descends slightly, but continuously, from the west to the
east. The soil is filled with green shrubbery, although the vegetation does not
rise very high, save a few lone trees. Travel through the plain would be easy,
and one can continue in any direction.
name: Scrubland

desc:
Between the winding slopes lies a small, flat plain with a circular pond in the
centre of it. Green, long grass rustle gently around the pool, some of it
trampled down by the animals wandering in this area. The ground level of the
adjacent areas is slightly higher, forming a natural dell. The bushy scrubland
continues in all directions but to the east, where the vegetation changes into
thick, wild-looking bushes.
name: Pond in the Plains

desc:
The northern edge of the wide scrubland is covered with a myriad of plants, such
as tall grasses and scraggely bushes. The ground is sloping slightly and there
is no sign of any kind of organised agriculture. The open scrubland stretches
onwards to the south and west, with the exception to the east, where the terrain
transfers into a long, abruptly-rising slope filled with thickets.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
The land is uncultivated and covered with sparse stunted undergrowth; the only
exceptions are the small clumps of bushy trees that are growing throughout the
expanse. Between these scattered copses, the vast, wild scrubland extend in all
directions, but to the south, where a huge amount of crude boulders, and dense,
almost-tangled foliage blocks further travel.
name: Scrubland

desc:
The wide scrubland is very flat and seems to extend many miles in all
directions. The ground appears dry, but still quite fertile. Thick, yellow grass
surrounds the holly trees, thistles, and other bushes occupying the area. Wild
weeds grow in abundance across the terrain mixed with a few small trees. Behind
the fields, at the eastern horizon, looms the dark wood of Mirkwood.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
Man-high, healthy looking bushes have occupied vast areas of the plain. Each
shrub stands alone, having a few yards distance from the other bushes, thus
making the travelling on the flat plain easy, but very unpredictable. The soil
seems fertile: the grass here is taller than in most surrounding areas, and even
some tiny, white flowers grow in small patches among it. The landscape is open
in all directions.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The terrain transforms to a peculiar dead end in form of a stony valley filled
with several jagged boulders. It encloses the area, preventing access everywhere
but to the east. The ground is rocky and shattered; many dips and hollows pierce
the earth around the area. In the middle of the valley, a huge, rectangular
boulder with sharp edges rests on top of few others, forming a room-like space
under it.
name: Stony Valley

desc:
The road runs through a desolated landscape. The terrain is rather forested,
trees and bushes of various types grow all around the road, especially large
black beeches grow here in abundance. North of the road, the wilderness looks
impenetrable, whereas the southern region is mainly covered by bushes and is
easy to travel. To the east, the mighty Mirkwood is quite close.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
A broad zone of bushes spreads between the plains and the great forest of
Mirkwood. The bushes are so high and so filled with branches that they do not
permit a clear line of sight; resulting in a confusing warren of vegetation
where it is easy to get lost. To the west, there is a bush filled slope with so
steep an inclination that it is impossible to go there at all.
name: Rambling Bushes

desc:
Diverse green shrubbery has occupied this area, forming a wide section of dense,
continuous thicket where bushes are rather tall and wide, rich with branches.
The soil is covered with leaves, weeds and other undergrowth. So thick is the
vegetation that it is possible to weave one's way through the copse only where
the brushwood allows it.
name: Thick, Confusing Bushes

desc:
Numerous spike bushes grow on the scrubland, along with a foot high dark green
weed. The thick, impenetrable-looking brushwood continues to the north, while to
the east the landscape is dominated by outskirts of the Mirkwood. To the south,
vegetation turns into grassy plains which seem to extend far into the horizon.
name: Scrublands

desc:
Broad, nearly level stretches of land seem to continue in every direction from
here. Tufted grasses fill the landscape, colouring it with a rich variety of
slightly alternating shades of brown, yellow and green. A few woody plants have
taken root among the spiky bushes that get taller to the eastward. Neither trees
nor boulders stick out from the general blandness.
name: Broad Plains

desc:
These plains are a part of a flat, almost treeless, grassy country. Small bushes
and other woody, rough plants, like briars and thistles, grow here. Green is the
predominant tone of the foliage of these vast grasslands, which extend in all
directions except west, where a steep escarpment, abruptly sinking more than ten
yards down, breaks the mostly even landscape.
name: Verdant Plains

desc:
A beautiful sea of modest heathers dominates the undergrowth, occupying most of
the land surface around the widely spaced, scattered trees. It is coloured with
a rich mixture of deep green leaves, brown stems, and fragile violet blossom. In
addition, blueberries and other woody plant species grow here in abundance,
complementing the scale with blue and more shades of green. Far to the north, a
dark green forest breaks into the horizon and the vegetation gets more bushy.
name: Heather Plains

desc:
A dense thorn barrier separates the scrubland from the dark, sinister Mirkwood.
The ground looks really messy with spiky shrubs mixed with some coarse grass
which grow all over the area. Everything is covered with broken twigs and fallen
leaves which are littering the ground. No signs of wildlife exist, making the
atmosphere silent and oppressing. The thorn barrier continues to the west.
name: Thorn Barrier

desc:
A thick, impenetrable thorn barrier follows the border line of the ancient
forest of the Mirkwood which lies just behind the thorns. The foreboding
presence of the forest hangs in the air and makes even the vegetation look
tainted and grim. Numerous decaying leaves cover the earth, colouring it with
dull shades of brown.
name: Thorn Barrier

desc:
A huge, solitary tree stands alone on a small mound; the only break in the vast
expanse of open plains extending to the horizon. Once a truly royal
representative of its species, the tree looks now just old and dried; its uneven
bark has cracked from several spots, and the branches are thin and spindly. A
large brown patch of dead earth surrounds the tree. There, a tiny plant
struggles to survive.
name: A Mighty, Parched Tree

desc:
The Mirkwood is dark, silent and dense. Huge, black trees loom next to each
other; their tangled branches, murky leaves and hanging vines forming a solid
canopy above that is preventing all the light and quickly suffocating all the
sounds. The ground is full of fallen, black leaves, totally hiding the soil
underneath them. A high thorn barrier runs throughout the area, encircling the
dark, silent trees. An uncountable amount of huge spikes is protruding outwards
from the barrier, as a warning of impending death for anyone who dares to
attempt to venture deeper into the forest.
name: Outskirts of Mirkwood

desc:
The nearer the Mirkwood gets, the denser the brush is. Spine bushes, brambles
and thistles grow in abundance forming a kind of natural passageway which arches
gently towards the west and south. To the north, there is a copse of bushes
which sticks out of its surroundings as the bushes have dried and died where
they stand. To the east and south, one can sense the presence of the dark, huge
forest.
name: Copse of Dried Bushes

desc:
The terrain is very challenging inside the dense thicket. Neither clear path nor
any kind of wider open area exists, which would make the smooth travelling in
this green shrubbery possible. Behind every bush there seems to be just another
copse waiting. It is difficult to keep track of directions here.
name: Tangled Bushes

desc:
There is a small and moist hole underneath the ground. The walls are near each
other and made of stones intertwined with roots and dried mud. Soft, white
mushrooms are growing on the walls in vast mats and are spreading their mycelia
everywhere. The floor consists of coarse gravel which is covered by leaves and
twigs. The air is very dense, almost overwhelmingly so, due to the spores of
fungi which fill the air.
name: In the Stump

desc:
Lush, green grass covers these plains for as far as the eye can see. The plains
continue south and eastward, seemingly without end. Many miles to the west, even
the most inexperienced traveller could spot the smoke and, if the wind was
favourable, smell the telltale signs of a village.
name: Vast Grasslands

desc:
The massive plains, so characteristic of the lands surrounding the Anduin River
far to the west, end abruptly here at a small grove of trees. As large and
unyielding as these plains appear to be, they are not so mighty as to withstand
the domination of man. Just to the west, a small man-made grove of elms stand as
cover for a large village just beyond.
name: End of the Grasslands

desc:
Though not as impressive as the walls surrounding some towns, the walls of this
village appear impossible to penetrate. On close inspection, it becomes clear
that these walls are not solid but live vines and brush intertwining live elm
trees. The brush has been woven so tightly that no light from inside the village
escapes.
name: Outside the Village Walls

desc:
There is an airy and surprisingly spacious pit under the giant stump. The
entrance is nicely camouflaged either by a strange coincidence of nature or by
cunning skill. The ceiling of this room is covered by numerous roots of all
sizes, while the walls and the floor are a mixture of hard sand, some trunks and
smooth-edged rocks. It remains unclear whether this space is used as a home, and
who or what would live here, under the roots.
name: Under the Roots

desc:
Massive trees and dark, dense vegetation border the age-old, sandy road running
through the forest. The road was originally several yards wide, but since then
the forest has overwhelmed it in several places, and only a narrow trail
remains. A tangled mess of leaves, ferns and branches grows around and above the
road forming a thick cover of vegetation and absorbing most of the light and
sounds to itself which creates an echoless and oppressive atmosphere.
name: Old Forest Trail

desc:
This is the place where the Old Forest Road meets the border of Mirkwood. The
huge, gloomy forest wall seems to continue endlessly, as ancient and malevolent
looking trees fulfil the view by towering into great heights. The edge of
Mirkwood is intact, save for a place where an arching tunnel mouth looms between
two massive, leaning tree trunks. An old, worn sign stands by the road, and
behind it the road disappears into the dark, silent foliage. Only a short way
into the forest an old tree has fallen recently and blocks the path with its
entangled crown.
name: The Entrance to Mirkwood

desc:
A trail of densely packed soil covered with gravel makes its way through high
grass and bushes. Roots break the carefully laid-out path from time to time. The
forest looms over the trail, with beech and oak growing very dense both in the
north and south. Darkness engulfs Mirkwood in all directions save to the east
and west, where the fictive safety of the trail continues.
name: Old Forest Trail

desc:
Some parts of the forest have been cleared of their once majestic trees. An old
trail makes its way through Mirkwood. Old stumps alongside the road are covered
in old, grey moss, and others infested with malicious fungi and pests. The
forest itself lies dense, but traversable, to the north. In the south it once
again grows tall, and the trees stand close, denying any possible way for
further exploring. The trail continues its slithering route through the heavy
undergrowth in the east and west.
name: Old Forest Trail

desc:
Stumps and roots interrupt the weather-beaten old trail, which was made by
pebbles and gravel being laid over densely packed black soil. The trail has no
defining borders, simply merging into the young trees of the forest on either
side. The woods grow unfriendly and twisted to the south, leaving only a small
passage available for further travel. To the north the trees grow in dense
lines, looming in a threatening manner over the pathway. The trail itself
continues its way through Mirkwood to the east and west.
name: Old Forest Trail

desc:
The trail rises a few feet here, having been built on a mound of dirt, as it
makes its way through the stout, tall trees. The terrain is relatively
undamaged. The few huge branches and tree trunks which have fallen lie covered
in vines, moss and various other plants. The menacing oak and beech lean over
the trail, shutting out the natural light efficiently with their crowns. The old
forest trail extends its arms to the east and west.
name: Old Forest Trail

desc:
Fallen branches and twigs lie scattered across the trail. Small pebbles and
decomposing vegetation lie in small mounds, serving as homes for various
insects. Tree stumps, covered by fungi and moss, lie dead alongside the pathway.
Low, forested hills rise tall in the south, engulfed in a dark, menacing gloom.
The trail itself continues its path to the east and west.
name: Old Forest Trail

desc:
A dense network of old trees blocks out the sky with their great crowns. The
majority of the darkened trees are covered by a damp, grey moss, which seems to
make the air even more humid. Many trees have fallen and lie rotting on the
ground to the south, creating a passageway to an old trail. The forest grows
close to the north and east.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The trees stand tall and dense, their majestic crowns block out any possible
natural light. Fungi and a variety of mosses cover the fallen as well as
standing flora, leaving many of the trunks and branches in a brittle state. The
forest is growing thicker in all directions, leaving only a narrow passage to
the south.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Great oaks reach towards the sky, leaning their great crowns against one
another, supporting each other's heavy weight. Together they create a natural
blanket blocking most outside light. The undergrowth is extremely dense in the
south, forming an impenetrable wall of vegetation. To the north Mirkwood opens
up slightly, sloping down towards a trail. It seems possible to travel through
the murky vegetation lying to the east.
name: Murky Forest

desc:
The skeleton of Mirkwood is slowly starting to wane. Old, rotten trees have
fallen to the ground, piling up in the east to form an impermeable wall. This
pile of decomposing vegetable matter has turned into a haven for cockroaches,
termites and various deteriorating fungi. Dense, heavy forest grows in the north
and west, while in the south a trail runs on its way through the woods.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Poison ivy reaches out from the trees, entangling fallen trees and decomposed
bushes. The stifling humidity makes the already foul air even harder to bear.
Some thick vines and roots have been cleared away to the east, leaving a small
trail which appears to lead between the bases of the trees. Darkened trunks
block any vision to the north and south, leaving weary travellers with just two
options for further exploration: down the slope eastwards, or deeper into the
dark forest in the west.
name: Deep inside Mirkwood

desc:
The sloping forest falls in from the west, with its trees poised over a myriad
of bushes, branches and other fallen vegetation. The ground is covered in a
dense mishmash of vines and shrubs. Roots and ivy create a natural roof, keeping
out most of the natural light. The shadowed forest continues to rise above you
in the south, while in the east it settles slightly. The view to the north is
completely blocked by the heavy vegetation.
name: Deep inside Mirkwood

desc:
Great vines and roots have taken over here, leaving the trees in despair.
Leaning towards each other they form a close-to-regular, knitted pattern,
shielding the lower forest from any outside light. An old animal's den has been
completely ruined by the abundant vegetation. The boulders and rocks by the
entrance seem to have frozen in time, forever stuck among the twisted roots. The
forest ivy climbs several feet up into the air towards the west, creating a
brittle roof.
name: Among the Roots

desc:
Poison ivy descends the trunks of the surrounding trees, together with roots and
decomposed trees creating a very cumbersome environment to explore. Similar
growth can be seen in the north. In the west the tangles reach for each other,
ensnaring each others' stems in midair, forming an impenetrable quilt. Like a
fortress wall, Mirkwood rises tall and strong to the south.
name: Among the Roots

desc:
Enormous roots of black oak trees rise high above the ground creating an
intimidating, tight meshwork, rendering the area extremely difficult to
traverse. A change in the structure of vegetation can be noticed to the east and
north, where vines and ivy seem to seek the forest floor. A steep, overgrown
slope blocks any further travel to the west while the imposing vegetation to the
south doesn't leave any obvious route to move forward.
name: Underneath the Forest

desc:
The great oaks and beech stand tall and proud, their leaves, branches and twigs
mingling together in a single mass like a patchwork quilt far up in the air.
Roots and vines entangle each other on the ground, as though refusing to reveal
any easy way to walk here. Great bushes move uneasily in the north, blocking out
any further view in that direction. The whole forest is engulfed in a sombre
darkness.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Rustling ivy and periwinkle evoke a sense of uneasiness in these forested hills.
The vines have engulfed several skeletons of great animals. A smell of decay and
death lies heavily on the air. Ruptured tree trunks open up a steep passage
further up onto the hills in the east. In the north the darkness leads into a
murky place.
name: Low Hills of Mirkwood

desc:
Beech, fir and great oaks grow very densely here. Their trunks rise high, aiming
their crowns toward the sky. Most natural light is efficiently blocked out by
the great Mirkwood canopy. A quick fall to the west leads any travellers through
ravaged trunks and vines deeper into the darkness. Downhill in the north and
east the aggressive vegetation leaves just enough space for weary travellers to
continue their exploration.
name: Low Hills of Mirkwood

desc:
The ground makes a steep descent to the south, leaving the peak of the hills
behind and leading into a dense growth of oak, fir and beech. Here the
surroundings are somewhat less overgrown, yet the forest canopy maintains its
density, blocking out all natural light. Just below a great oak tree, a large
bird's nest has fallen and been looted, and is now slowly decaying, a pile of
debris. A slope full of malicious vegetation leads up westwards, deeper into the
gloom.
name: Low Hills of Mirkwood

desc:
The hills are low, with darkened trees leaning over one another. Fir, beech and
oak alternate in regular patterns, together building almost solid walls of trees
-- as though on purpose... The heavy, thick roots of fallen trunks intertwine
with hanging vines to make the going treacherous, with almost no clear way
through for the average traveller. A steep slope to the north climbs into yet
more forested hills, while to the west the forest seems slighly lower.
name: Low Hills of Mirkwood

desc:
An almost fortified forest rises up to the south and east, standing tall and
proud. Decomposing vegetation lies scattered over the forest floor, making
further travel quite demanding, and the malicious vines hanging down from the
branches of the trees leave little room to move. To the north the heavy forest
covers more treacherous hills.
name: Murky Hills of Mirkwood

desc:
Vines and roots create a forest floor shaped like a pit filled with serpents.
Shadows of branches, trunks and roots form a complex pattern on the ground. Ivy
is hanging down from the tops of the fir and oak, making this area fairly
demanding to explore. A slight uphill slope in the east leads from this
entangled area into a gloomy forest. Heavily forested hills rise in the south.
The vicious underbrush broadens its space to the north, creating a natural
tunnel, deep underneath the trees.
name: Among the Roots

desc:
Oak and beech stand close together, interconnected by periwinkle and ground ivy.
The forest has completely dehydrated the very soil it stands on, leaving the
forest floor dry, brittle and dusty. The imposing darkness may be further
explored in the south and east, while the north is completely blocked by the
dense trees, making it impossible to venture in that direction.
name: Murky Forest

desc:
Fir and beech spread their majestic crowns through the air, efficiently
preventing most natural light from seeping through. Ravaged ground-ivy and
wisteria lie lifeless on the ground. Further up in the trees fungi and mosses
have almost overwhelmed the very trees they get their nourishment from. A slope
descends down to the north, leading deeper into the murky forest of Mirkwood.
name: Low Hills of Mirkwood

desc:
Great birch and beech gather in a great collaborative effort, interweaving their
branches and roots to form an impassable wall in the south and west. A grand
canopy, reinforced by periwinkle and poison ivy, effectively closes out any form
of natural light. The hills appear to come to an end in the east, while in the
north a steep slope leads deeper into the malicious forest of Mirkwood.
name: Murky, Forested Hills

desc:
To the east a small entrance seems to have been purposefully opened between the
great roots of the trees. To the west the forest appears more open and more
readily accessible. An uneasy rustling stemming from the heavy vegetation,
together with the twisted poison ivy and darkened roots, gives the forest an
eerie aura. In the south the forest opens up into ancient, wide plains.
name: Before the Forest Wall

desc:
The light brown earth here is home to patches of grass growing no more than two
feet tall. A burned old farm cart lies in line with an old skeleton of what must
have been an ass or a horse. Far in the east a small trail appears to lead up to
a house; the deserted meadows expand to the south and west. The forest of
Mirkwood stands proud in the north.
name: Deserted Meadows

desc:
The salty, dehydrated ground is littered with pebbles and boulders. Nothing can
be seen growing for several leagues in all directions, except for the great
forest of Mirkwood to the north. The forest springs out of the ground like a
compact castle wall, and no obvious way in can be seen from here. The famished
land stretches further into vast plains to the south, west and east.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
A dusty path almost wholly covered in tussocks of grass lies stretched across
the field. Westward it can barely be made out among the meadows and quickly
disappears in the earthy ground. To the south its contours become more apparent
as the grass grows less abundant. To the north and east the mighty trees of
Mirkwood have created an impassable wall of twisted trunks and vines.
name: A Path in the Fields

desc:
The lifeless plains stretch as far as the eye can see, except for the north
where the mighty forest of Mirkwood rises up much like a great wall. The trees
stand aligned, with enormous bushes and shrubs growing between the trunks,
leaving no gaps through which to pass. The ground is covered with rocks of
various sizes, and the dirt on which they lie is slightly white, resembling
salt. Nothing in remote need of nutrients would be able to grow here.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
The trees of Mirkwood stand aligned with one another, reaching for the sky.
Their crowns unfold far up in the air, covering every inch of the sky, leaving
the natural light outside. Across the forest floor decaying vines, twigs and
bushes spread an odour of death. The dark forest opens up to more
readily-accessible woods to the north and south.
name: Sinister Forest

desc:
Oak and beech stand tall and proud, their crowns cooperatively forming a vast
canopy, stretching far up into the air. Darkened vines and ivy encircle their
trunks, while heavy vegetation of bushes and roots cover their bases. The ground
is covered in decomposing leaves and twigs. The aggressive vegetation allows for
little exploration, leaving only the north accessible.
name: Sinister Forest

desc:
The threatening, malevolent forest is dark. The sky above cannot be seen through
the net of thick branches, thus eternal darkness encompasses the seemingly
endless, massive trees that tower eerily into the great heights. Tangled,
half-rotten vines hang gloomily down from the twisted tree limbs, here and there
intertwining with the feral, unhealthy looking black bushes. A stench of death
hangs heavily in the air.
name: Sinister Forest

desc:
The terrain is cumbersome here. Ferns spread across the forest floor underneath
tall fir and beech, and cobwebs stretch through the air between the old, gnarled
branches of this ancient forest. The tall undergrowth impedes freedom of
movement, while offering a perfect hunting ground for such carnivores as are
suited to this enviornment. Rotten cocoons hang from a few trees, cracked open
and emptied. Nearby lie half-eaten carcasses, infested by larvae. A stench of
death spreads across the area.
name: Murky Ferns of Mirkwood

desc:
The air is dark, heavy and thick under the malevolent trees, which seem to lean
towards travellers, stretching out to reach them. The movement is difficult
because of the giant roots and branches that are intertwined everywhere with
each other. And where the nature has not blocked the way, sticky, silvery spider
webs have been woven between the trunks.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The forest here is marked by a meshwork of twisting vines, interweaving the
alive with the dead. The dense vegetation creates tall natural walls,
purposefully encircling anyone trying to explore it. The trees loom over one
another, stopping the sun and stars from casting their light into Mirkwood.
name: Deep inside Mirkwood

desc:
The darkness surrounds, even engulfs this area. The solid canopy of the forest
hangs above with heavy branches growing downwards as other free directions do
not exist. So thick is the ceiling made of wood and leaves that daylight
probably never shines on this feral place. Some stones lie on the ground; their
edges half concealed by moss and ferns creating treacherous places to stumble
upon. A deathlike silence pervades the whole forest.
name: Dark, Oppressive Forest

desc:
The forest does not look healthy here. Some of the big trees are clearly rotten,
and they look like they may fall down at any moment. A few trees have broken and
collapsed already, lying among the vegetation as a part of the decayed, smelly
mess that covers the ground. Above, the upper part of branches have grown
compactly together, making the forest very dark.
name: Rotten Forest by the Road

desc:
The black, shadowy forest looms in all directions as far as one can see. The
trees are massive, and the big tree trunks are easily more than two yards thick.
The ceiling above is formed by the forest itself, blocking the sky totally out
of the field of vision. Branches twist to and fro, covered by the seemingly
endless vines. The undergrowth looks half rotten, complementing the rather scary
and oppressing atmosphere this place has.
name: Sinister, Still Forest

desc:
Grand oaks and beech create a shadowy forest, rising from a brittle, dry ground.
The great roots and vines have sucked the life out of the earthy forest floor. A
great bird's nest has fallen to the ground, broken up by pests and putrid fungi.
The dense vegetation, combined with the presence of death, bury Mirkwood in a
sombre darkness. The dark forest lies explorable to the south, west and east.
name: Murky Forest

desc:
The surrounding forest stands tall. The fir, beech and oak grow close together,
and their heavy branches and wide crowns engulf Mirkwood in darkness. An old
bear's den has been dug out below the giant roots of an oak, exposing several
cub skeletons. A heavy stench of decay encompasses the area, reinforced by the
moist air. The forest floor slowly rises upwards to the south, leading into
heavily forested hills, while in the north and west the tree trunks and
undergrowth are completely engulfed in darkness.
name: Murky Forest

desc:
Under and between the black roots, a narrow tunnel snakes its way in the
darkness. The surrounding roots are thick and gnarled, seemingly penetrating
deeper and deeper to the ground to suck any life force there still may be. The
black soil around the roots is humid and rather cold. The tunnel ends to a dead
end to the west, and leads forward to the east.
name: Dead End in the Darkness

desc:
The entrance to the tunnel is cramped and damp. The walls and ceiling are of
hard-packed soil and rocks. Wooden poles maintain the structure of the tunnel.
Traces of cobweb and nesting spiders can be seen among rocks and other debris.
The tunnel twists and turns northwards.
name: Entrance to a Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is in a deteriorated state. Wooden poles lie scattered across the
floor. Some of the beams in the ceiling have started to split open. The turning
tunnel leads to the west and south, the dark, packed soil to the south giving
way to stone in the west. The air in the cavern is humid and lukewarm, and there
is a a smell of death and decay. Small humanoid bones lie spread out among
fallen rocks and boulders.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is in complete disorder here. It slopes downwards to the east, big
rocks and wooden bars covering most of the floor and leaving quite a difficult
route to travel. The tunnel comes to an end at a collapsed shaft. A vast cobweb
spans the ceiling, filled with moths and other small bugs. Among the debris one
can discern skulls, ribs and other bones, many gnawed upon.
name: Collapsed Tunnel

desc:
There are three obvious exits at this nexus of tunnels: one leading to the west,
another descending down towards the north, and a dangerous-looking old shaft
leading upwards. Great spider webs span across the ceiling, along parts of the
walls, from stalagmite to stalagmite and from stalactite to stalactite.
Decomposing cocoons hang from the webs. Seemingly fresh traces of blood and a
drained, yet not rotten, body are clear evidence of recent activity. A sour
smell engulfs the entire area, irritating skin and eyes. The humid, lukewarm air
makes a good habitat for the fungi which have started to take over the cave.
name: Abandoned Spider's Nest

desc:
A seemingly small underground spring creates a pitch-black pool at the furthest
side of the cave. A rancid smell stems from the water. There are remnants of
smaller animals floating about in the pool. Great stalactites hang from the
ceiling. Webs and cocoons span across the cavern in the south, the cave system
continuing beyond them.
name: Underground Spring

desc:
Stalagmites and stalacites encompass the whole cavern. A narrow crack in the
ground between tall stalagmites emits a stinging stench. Fungi and moss cover
parts of the walls and crevices. Webs span between the pointy stalacites in the
ceiling, some in better shape than others. The tunnel continues to the east; the
only other exit is down through the crack into the putrid water below.
name: End of a Tunnel

desc:
The water is very cold and vision is limited. Something in the water stings the
skin and eyes. The cavern walls are marked by many small pit-holes, suitable
perhaps for hiding captured prey -- or concealing predators? The walls are bare,
with not a trace even of weeds or moss. There is a small crack in the south side
of the ceiling, leaving a possible passage of escape.
name: Submerged Cavern

desc:
Ground ivy makes the forest floor close to impossible to explore. Moving about
requires precise navigation and care. A slope leads slightly upwards in the
east, through ravaged trees and vines. A stench of decay lies heavy over the
area. The dense Mirkwood forest expands its kingdom to the south, into yet
darker, more sombre areas.
name: Low Hills of Mirkwood

desc:
Mirkwood grows less dense here, yet the crowns of the trees manage to keep the
darkness consistent, while a heavy moisture seems to weigh down the lukewarm
air. Various forms of underbrush grow on the forest floor, intertwined with
poison ivy. The ivy appears to nurture itself from the beech and fir on which it
climbs. The forest opens up towards an old trail in the north, and to a
malicious pit of roots and vines in the west.
name: Low Hills of Mirkwood

desc:
A shattered well stands in the centre of the square of this village. The posts
of the well have been broken in half, the roof burnt, and the bricks pulled out
of place. Beneath the watchtower that used to protect this village lies the
trapdoor that sat at the top of the watchtower's ladder. Both the trapdoor and
the ladder have been damaged, but the ladder is only charred while all that
remains of the trapdoor are the metal straps that used to hold in place. On the
north of the square lies a razed forge while to the west there once stood a
house.
name: Inside a Razed Village

desc:
A pig's head is impaled on the northern post that made the frame of the broken
western gate. On the ground there are two broken troughs, the water and fodder
has mixed with pigs blood turning it into a red porridge. The wooden palisade is
still solid enough to block all travel to the south and east. The barn north of
here has been razed, and to the west the village is in just as bad a condition.
name: A Razed Pasture

desc:
The ashes are still smouldering in what was once a barn. The fire must have
blazed fiercely when it passed through here. Covered in ashes on the ground are
burnt animal corpses. Their meat has been burned off and they are now inedible.
A charred palisade runs beside the barn to the north and east and it still
blocks any exit. West of here there is a razed forge and to the south are the
remains of a wooded pasture.
name: A Razed Barn

desc:
Not much remains of what once was a forge. The only thing still standing intact
here is a blackened and battered old anvil. Scattered among the other pieces of
debris are partly melted tools. The fire here must have fed on the forge's fuel
to reach a temperature hot enough to damage even the lowest quality tools. North
of here the burnt but partly intact palisade still prevents people from leaving
or entering the village that way.
name: A Razed Forge

desc:
A fire has raged without mercy here. The only things that are not totally burnt
to the ground are the logs that make up the wooden palisade, and they have taken
quite a bit of fire damage. This small village has been badly hurt by fire, and
little of this house remains. East of here there is a razed forge and to the
south there is more burnt debris.
name: A Burnt Down House

desc:
Burnt and blackened logs are scattered about among the ashes on the ground. An
ash-covered chimney made of stone is the only thing still standing erect. To the
north there is only debris and ashes while to the west and south lie the burnt
remains of the palisade. East of here is the regular entrance to this destroyed
village.
name: A Burnt Down House

desc:
For those of Beorning descent, this cave feels right at home, bringing some of
the wild in a more civilised settlement. The walls of the cave feature mixed
shades of brown and grey; and are relatively clean. Very little litter can be
found, signs that this dwelling is well-maintained. A fresh pile of leaves can
be found in the back of the cave, marking it as the resting place of the cave's
inhabitant. Sticky remains of honeycomb are spread on the cavern wall along with
large claw marks. A neat pile of bones, most are broken in half and were abused
by sharp teeth.
name: Faine's Lair

desc:
A small and simple stall with a neat look stands to the west. The smell of fresh
hay, and some occasional loud yet mild neighs reach you from behind its coarse
gate, where, half-hidden in shadows, you glimpse heaps of golden yellow straw. A
little path, that comes running through wide gardens from the southern end of
the house, approaches near here before bending eastwards, to the encounter of
the main one that leads into the courtyard.
name: In Front of a Barn

desc:
The low hills are slowly turning into a flatter landscape. The uneven, dark soil
appears nutritious, as the flora here is rich. Beech and oak grow rather
densely, yet leave enough room for bushes and vines to claim their presence. The
hills continue their expansion in the north, while the south leads deeper into
the dark forest of Mirkwood.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The birch and oak reach to staggering heights in the west and south, creating a
fortified, natural wall. The barely discernible north-south trail makes a turn
here, leading east along the forest barrier. A great spider web spans across two
tree-tops. It is littered with dead insects and moths of various sizes. The
remains of a small animal have fallen to the ground, the skeleton almost
completely buried in the dirt. A stench of decay encompasses the whole forest.
To the north the forest opens up into some dark hills.
name: A Dark Corner of Mirkwood

desc:
A pitch black, dead silent maze makes it way through the heavy vegetation. It is
supported by the roots of the great forest above, intertwined by poison ivy and
vines. Mirkwood appear to shift, slowly but constantly. There are no obvious
landmarks to orient onself with, and the area is limited for further
exploration.
name: Underneath the Forest

desc:
There is a cramped, open space between the black mother rock and numerous sharp
stalactites. The stone wall is cold and wet, and some shimmering veins are
faintly visible on its surface. The ground is filled with numerous rocks, gravel
and stones. At one spot the stalactites are located outwards, allowing one to
squeeze through them.
name: Behind the Stalactites
